# Confidence Heatmap — Framework v0.3

*Generated by `scripts/confidence_heatmap.py` on 2026-04-30.*

Confidence is the rater's degree of certainty in each (language × dimension) score, set when the evaluation YAML was authored. **High** means the score is well-anchored in primary sources and would be unlikely to move on review; **medium** means the score is anchored but could move 0.5; **low** means the score depends on judgment that another rater could reasonably challenge.

Cohort: 10 languages × 7 dimensions = 70 cells.

- High confidence: 19 cells (27%)
- Medium confidence: 51 cells (73%)
- Low confidence: 0 cells (0%)

## Heatmap

| Language | HC | MC | AO | RE | SV | AI | SO |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| TypeScript | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ● high | ● high | ● high | ● high |
| Python | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ● high | ● high | ● high | ● high |
| Go | ● high | ◐ med | ◐ med | ● high | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med |
| .NET (C#) | ◐ med | ● high | ◐ med | ● high | ● high | ● high | ◐ med |
| Rust | ◐ med | ● high | ◐ med | ● high | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med |
| Kotlin | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ● high | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med |
| Java | ◐ med | ● high | ◐ med | ● high | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med |
| Elixir | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med |
| Swift | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med |
| C++ | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med | ◐ med |

**Dimension keys:** HC = Human Cognition · MC = Machine Cognition · AO = AI-Agent Operability · RE = Runtime/Ecosystem · SV = Strategic Viability · AI = AI-systems Interoperability · SO = Structured-output Maturity.

## How to read this

A row of high-confidence cells means the language's evaluation is well-anchored. A row with multiple medium cells indicates dimensions where another rater could reasonably arrive at a half-point different score. Low-confidence cells mark places where the framework's evidence base is thinnest and where additional source work would have the most leverage.

## Observations

**Top-tier languages are best-anchored.** TypeScript, Python, and .NET each have 4 high-confidence cells, concentrated on RE/SV/AI/SO — the dimensions where ecosystem evidence is strongest. The agentic axis (AO) is medium across the board, reflecting that operability scoring depends on judgment about agent workflows that has thinner objective measurement.

**Runtime/ecosystem is the most-anchored dimension.** 7 of 10 languages have RE=high. The exceptions — Elixir, Swift, C++ — sit at medium because each has unusual structural properties (BEAM model, Apple-platform-specific maturity, accelerator-first dialect divergence) that make a direct comparison harder.

**Human cognition has only one high-confidence cell** — Go, where the language's deliberate minimalism is sharply documented. Every other HC score is medium, which is honest: readability is the dimension where rater priors carry the most weight.

**No cell is at low confidence.** Every score in the matrix has enough source-anchored support to defend at "medium or better." This reflects the corpus-based evidence approach but also flags that the framework has not yet stress-tested its scores against an adversarial review — see `outputs/v0.3-review-pass.md` for the formal review pass.

## Refreshing

The matrix in `comparisons/overview.md` and the per-language scores in `evaluations/<lang>.yaml` are the upstream source. Re-run `python scripts/confidence_heatmap.py` after any score change to refresh this file.
